nodes.label: AI for subjective tasks (rewriting, interpreting)
nodes.grounding_span: I like to use AI for more subjective things (like phrasing or rewriting)

nodes.label: AI accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: AI models tend to hallucinate instead of admitting they can't find something

nodes.label: AI as efficiency booster for tedious tasks
nodes.label_negative: Manual effort for organization and formatting
nodes.grounding_span: Rather than having to type everything out and organize it myself, I just gave the AI most of the workouts

nodes.label: AI as skill dependency risk
nodes.grounding_span: It is possible to lose practice with any skill because of AI

nodes.label: AI future accuracy improvement
nodes.grounding_span: AI is only going to improve and become more accurate in the future

nodes.label: Personal judgment and control
nodes.grounding_span: I can make the final decision if I want to incorporate the AI's advice or not

nodes.label: Accuracy and trustworthiness
nodes.grounding_span: the accuracy of statistics and context is extremely important

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: helps them get tasks done better and more efficiently

nodes.label: Preference for AI in subjective tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I like to use AI for more subjective things

nodes.label: Distrust of AI for objective accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I trust myself to find accurate and relevant information more than AI

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: I quickly became frustrated because I could tell almost all of the information I was being given was hallucinated

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for organization
nodes.grounding_span: I used AI to help create a workout plan for a month, and that was pretty helpful

nodes.label: Concern about skill loss due to AI
nodes.grounding_span: It is possible to lose practice with any skill because of AI

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I see myself using AI more for tasks I consider to be objectively based in the future

nodes.label: Subjective-objective task differentiation
nodes.grounding_span: I like to use AI for more subjective things (like phrasing or rewriting) ... I trust myself more with the accuracy

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I will need to go back and forth with the AI to get a better suggestion or give it more context
